info.memory_trick: mono（单一）+ ton（音调）+ ous（形容词后缀）→ 只有一个音调 → 单调的
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, but the daily routine there feels monotonous.

info.memory_trick: mono（单一）+ poly（多，此处为‘销售’变体）→ 单一卖家控制多数市场 → 垄断
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he holds a monopoly on patience in our study group.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'primus'（第一）和 'principalis'（首要的），是印欧语系中表示“最前面、最初”的核心词根。它在英语中衍生出大量与“首要、原始、基础”相关的词汇，体现了时间或等级上的优先性。

info.definition: 主要的；首要的；最佳的；最初的
info.memory_trick: prime = prim（第一）+ e → 第一的，就是“首要的”；想象“prime minister”（首相）是国家的第一人物。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a prime advantage in his career.

info.memory_trick: primary = prim（第一）+ ary（形容词后缀）→ 第一的，即“主要的”或“初级的”；联想“primary school”（小学）是教育的第一阶段。
info.example: The primary goal of the project is to improve efficiency, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps in international collaboration.

info.memory_trick: primitive = prim（第一）+ itive（形容词后缀）→ 第一阶段的，即“原始的”；想象原始人使用简陋工具，是最初的人类形态。
info.example: The primitive tools found in the cave date back thousands of years, but he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, contrasting with the slow evolution of early communication.

info.definition: 主要的；首要的；校长；本金
info.memory_trick: principal = prin（第一）+ cip（拿，取）+ al（形容词/名词后缀）→ 拿第一的，即“首要的”；联想学校“principal”（校长）是学校的第一负责人。注意与 principle 区别：principal 是“人”或“首要的”，principle 是“原则”。
info.example: The principal reason for his success is his dedication, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making him a top candidate for the international post.

info.memory_trick: principle = prin（第一）+ cip（拿）+ le（名词后缀）→ 首先要拿住的东西，即“原则”；可以记作“principle”（原则）是行为的首要准则，与 principal（校长）发音相同但拼写不同：末尾是 le 而非 al。
info.example: He sticks to the principle that practice makes perfect, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, applying this principle to every new skill.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 'videre'（看），其过去分词形式 'visus' 衍生出 'vis' 词根。核心含义为“看、观察”，引申为“可见的、明显的、证据”等概念，在英语中构成大量与视觉、认知相关的词汇。

info.memory_trick: e（出）+ vid（看）+ ence（名词后缀）→ 看得出来的东西 → 证据
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: e（出）+ vid（看）+ ent（形容词后缀）→ 看得出来的 → 明显的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语inter（在…之间）和prehendere（抓住、拿取）的组合。在拉丁语中，inter- + prehendere逐渐融合为interprehendere，后经古法语进入英语，演变为enter- + pris。核心含义是“在中间拿取”，引申为“参与、抓住注意力、包含”等。

info.memory_trick: enter（在…之间）+ prise（拿取）→ 在众多机会之间拿取，即开创事业所需的精神。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes him a valuable asset to any enterprise.

info.memory_trick: enter（在…之间）+ tain（源自tenere，拿取）→ 在人与人之间拿取注意力，即娱乐招待；或拿取想法于心中，即怀有。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often entertains his friends with his polyglot skills.

info.memory_trick: entertain（娱乐）+ ment（名词后缀）→ 娱乐的行为或事物。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he considers language learning a form of entertainment.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语词根 'licere'，意为‘被允许、可自由行动’。该词根衍生出与‘允许、自由、解放’相关的词汇，如 license（许可）、liberal（自由的）、liberate（解放），体现了从‘被允许’到‘自由状态’的语义扩展。

info.definition: n. 许可证，执照；v. 许可，批准
info.memory_trick: lic（允许）+ ense（名词后缀）→ 允许的凭证 → 许可证。联想‘lic’像‘lice（虱子）’，但许可证是‘允许’你摆脱麻烦。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still needs a license to teach them.

info.definition: adj. 自由的，开明的；慷慨的，丰富的
info.memory_trick: liber（自由）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 自由的。拆分‘liber’为‘lib（图书馆）+ er（人）’，图书馆给人自由获取知识的权利。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a liberal skill that opens many doors.

info.memory_trick: liber（自由）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使自由。联想‘liber’像‘liver（肝脏）’，解放就像给肝脏‘自由’排毒。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which can liberate his mind from cultural barriers.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 ligare（绑、系），引申为“约束、连接”，是法律、责任相关词汇的核心词根。

info.definition: 负有法律责任的；有义务的；易受……影响的
info.memory_trick: li（绑）+ able（能够）→ 能够被绑住→ 负有责任的
info.example: We must abolish the outdated regulations that make companies liable for accidents they couldn't prevent.

info.memory_trick: li（绑）+ ability（能力）→ 被绑住的能力→ 必须承担的责任
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to manage financial liability, turning debts into assets.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'viria'（手镯、环），后演变为法语 'viron'（周围），表示环绕、包围或环境的概念。该词根常出现在与'周围环境'或'循环'相关的词汇中，强调事物被包围或环绕的状态。

info.definition: 环境；周围状况；生态环境
info.memory_trick: en（进入）+ viron（环，周围）+ ment（名词后缀）→ 进入环绕的事物中 → 环境。想象自己站在一个巨大的环中，周围的一切就是你的环境。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'fundus'，意为底部、基础。引申为事物的根基、起点或根本原则。

info.memory_trick: fund = 方(fun)的地(d) —— 把资金投放在一个方形的基地上。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still needs a fund to support his studies abroad.

info.definition: 基本的，根本的；基本原理
info.memory_trick: fund（基础）+ a（一个）+ mental（精神的）—— 基础的精神核心，即根本的。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is fundamental to his success as a polyglot.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'novus'，意为 '新的、新鲜的'。该词根在英语中常表示与 '新' 相关的概念，如创新、革新、新颖等。

info.definition: n. 创新，革新；新方法，新事物
info.memory_trick: in（进入）+ nov（新）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 引入新东西 → 创新
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词'struere'（建造、堆叠）及其过去分词'structus'。该词根核心含义为'建造'或'构造'，引申出组织、建立、破坏等抽象概念。

info.memory_trick: struct（建造）+ ure（名词后缀）→ 建造出来的东西 → 结构/建筑物
info.example: The building has a steel structure that can withstand earthquakes.

info.memory_trick: con（共同）+ struct（建造）→ 共同建造 → 构建，建造
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to construct complex theories from simple observations.

info.memory_trick: de（向下/去除）+ struct（建造）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 把建造的东西拆掉 → 破坏
info.example: The earthquake caused widespread destruction across the city.

info.memory_trick: in（进入）+ struct（建造）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 把知识建造到脑海里 → 教导/指令
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, following the instruction carefully.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 socius，意为‘同伴、盟友、伙伴’，引申为‘联合、交往、结社’。该词根强调人与人之间的合作、联系和共同关系，是英语中表示‘社交、联合’类词汇的核心来源。

info.definition: v. 联想，联系；交往，结交；n. 伙伴，同事；adj. 副的，非正式的
info.memory_trick: as（加强）+ soci（同伴）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使成为同伴 → 联想、交往。想象你和一个同伴一起学习新语言，就会把单词和场景‘联想’起来。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and often associates with native speakers to practice.

info.definition: n. 协会，社团；联合，关联；联想
info.memory_trick: associate（联合）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 联合的状态或组织 → 协会。好比一群同伴联合起来组成一个学习协会，共同快速掌握新语言。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which led to his invitation to join the language learning association.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 'fungi'（执行、完成、履行），其过去分词 'functus' 演变为词根 'funct'，核心含义为 '执行功能、发挥作用'。在英语中常引申为 '功能、运作、职务' 等概念。

info.memory_trick: 词根 'funct' 意为'执行'，加上名词后缀 '-ion'，合起来就是'执行出来的东西'→功能。想象一个机器在执行它的任务（function）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 词根'ful'源自拉丁语'plenus'（充满），与英语'fill'（装满）同源，两者结合强调'使完全充满'的状态，引申为'实现、履行'，暗示将某事物填满至圆满完成。

info.definition: 实现；履行；满足（愿望、要求等）
info.memory_trick: 想象一个杯子（fill）被倒满（ful）到溢出，代表任务或承诺被完全'装满'，即完成。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he hopes to fulfil his dream of becoming a translator.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'stridere'，原意为‘发出刺耳声’或‘跨步前进’，后引申为‘大步行走、跨越’的动作感，强调步伐的幅度与力量。

info.memory_trick: 想象一个运动员在赛场上‘跨’出巨大的一步，嘴里喊着‘strid’的音，就像在说‘使劲跨’！
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'modus'，意为‘方式、尺度、模式’，后经古法语进入英语，最初指‘心境、情绪状态’，进而引申为语法中的‘语气’，表示说话者对动作或状态的态度或方式。

info.definition: 情绪，心情；语气（语法中表示说话者对动作态度的动词形式）
info.memory_trick: 谐音‘木的’——木头一样呆滞的表情，反映一种低落的情绪。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, regardless of his mood.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'staurare'（放置、储存），后经古法语 'estore' 进入英语，核心含义为‘储备、积累、保存’，引申出‘库存、商店、存储’等概念。

info.definition: n. 存货，库存；股票；高汤；v. 储备，供应
info.memory_trick: 想象一个仓库里堆满了‘stock’（存货），就像股票市场的‘stock’也是被‘储存’的资产。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he stocks up on vocabulary every day.

info.definition: n. 商店；储存物；v. 储存，贮藏
info.memory_trick: 商店（store）就是用来‘储存’商品的地方，把东西‘store’起来。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he stores every new word in his memory.

info.definition: n. 储存，保管；仓库，储藏室
info.memory_trick: storage = store + age，把东西‘store’起来，经历‘age’（时间）就是‘storage’（储存）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he uses digital storage to keep his notes organized.

info.origin: 源自希腊语 'psyche'（意为灵魂、心灵、精神），后经拉丁语进入英语，构成许多与心理、精神相关的词汇。

info.memory_trick: psych（心灵）+ ology（学科）→ 研究心灵的学科 → 心理学
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a fascinating subject for psychology.

info.memory_trick: psych（精神）+ iatry（治疗）→ 治疗精神疾病的学科 → 精神病学
info.example: Advances in psychiatry have greatly improved the treatment of mental disorders.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'sistere'，意为‘站立、使站立、停止’。该词根强调‘稳定存在’或‘持续处于某状态’，引申为‘保持不变’或‘存在于某处’。在英语中，它常与介词或前缀结合，形成表示‘由…组成’、‘一致’或‘安慰’等概念的词汇。

info.memory_trick: con（共同）+ sist（站立）→ 各部分‘共同站立’在一起，即‘组成’一个整体。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which consists of both innate talent and diligent practice.

info.definition: 一致的；始终如一的；连续的
info.memory_trick: con（共同）+ sist（站立）+ ent（形容词后缀）→ 各部分‘共同站立’不倾斜，即‘一致的、稳定的’。
info.example: His consistent effort in language learning, as shown in his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, earned him great respect.

info.memory_trick: con（共同）+ sole（独自？实为sist变体）→ 正确的联想：con（一起）+ sol（安慰，源于solari）+ e，但词根为sist时，可理解为‘使某人站在自己身边’以给予安慰。更简便：con（共同）+ sol（太阳）+ e → 太阳共同照耀，带来温暖和安慰。
info.example: He tried to console his friend, who lacked the remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, by offering study tips.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'malus'（坏）的衍生，后经古英语 'yfel' 演变而来，核心含义为道德上的邪恶、有害或不幸，与 'good'（好）相对，常引申为一切负面、恶劣的事物或行为。

info.definition: 邪恶的，有害的；罪恶，恶行
info.memory_trick: 谐音记忆：'一窝' → 一窝坏蛋聚在一起干坏事，就是邪恶的。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 amare（爱），引申为‘使人愉悦、吸引’等含义。词根 am 在英语中常出现在与情感、娱乐相关的词汇中，体现‘爱’带来的积极感受。

info.definition: 逗乐；使发笑；给…提供娱乐
info.memory_trick: a（一个）+ muse（缪斯女神）→ 缪斯女神带来灵感，也带来欢乐，让人开心。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his humorous stories often amuse his friends.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'mendare'，意为‘改正缺陷、修理损坏’，与 'menda'（瑕疵）同源；强调从错误或不完美状态恢复到正常或正确状态。

info.definition: 修正，修改（法律、文件等）；改善，改正（行为、状况等）
info.memory_trick: 词根mend意为‘修理’，a-加强前缀→‘加强修理’即‘修正、改善’。联想：a（一个）+ mend（修理）→ 一个需要修理的东西被修正好了。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he needs to amend his study habits to achieve better results.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'mont-'（山），及古法语 'monter'（上升），引申为数量或程度的累积与提升，如‘amount’表示总量或数值的上升。

info.memory_trick: 联想‘a mount（一座山）’——数量堆成山，就是总量。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 nocere（伤害）及其否定形式 innocens（无害的）。noc- 为词干表示“伤害”，innoc- 由否定前缀 in- + noc- 构成，意为“不伤害、无害”。该词根在英语中常引申为“无害的、无辜的”等含义。

info.definition: 无辜的；清白的；无害的；天真的
info.memory_trick: in-（不）+ noc（伤害）+ -ent（形容词后缀）→ 不伤害别人的 → 无辜的、天真的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'amplus'，意为‘大的、宽敞的、充足的’。该词根常出现在与‘规模、容量、数量’相关的词汇中，强调超出平均或标准的状态。

info.definition: 充足的，充裕的；宽敞的，宽大的
info.memory_trick: ampl（大）+ e → 空间或数量很大 → 充足的，宽敞的。想象一个巨大的仓库（ample warehouse），里面堆满了货物。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 词根 'among' 源自古英语 'on gemang'，意为 '在人群/混合中'，表示处于多个事物之间；后缀 '-st' 是古英语中的加强成分，用于强调位置或状态的稳固性。组合后 'amongst' 强化了 '在…之中' 的含义，常带有一种被包围或置身于群体中的感觉。

info.definition: 在…之中；在…中间（尤指被环绕或处于群体中）
info.memory_trick: 想象你站在一群人（among）中间，脚下有稳固的台阶（st）支撑，强调你确实身处其中。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which sets him apart among his peers.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语'constare'，由'com-'（一起）和'stare'（站立）组成，字面意为'站在一起'，引申为'保持不变、稳定、一致'。

info.definition: adj. 恒定的；不变的；持续的；n. 常数
info.memory_trick: con（一起）+stant（站立）= 一直站在一起 → 恒定不变
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his dedication remains constant.

info.definition: n. 成分；选民；adj. 构成的；组成的
info.memory_trick: con（一起）+stitut（站立）+ent（人/物）= 站在一起组成整体 → 成分、构成部分
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a key constituent of his linguistic success.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语前缀dis-，表示‘分离、分开、否定、相反’。在拉丁语中常与动词结合，强调‘使分开’或‘取消’的动作。例如dis- + ordere（秩序）→ disorder（混乱，即秩序被分离）。该前缀在英语中广泛用于构成表示‘相反、移除、分散’的词汇。

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ order（秩序）→ 秩序被分离 → 混乱
info.example: The room was in complete disorder after the party.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ sperse（撒播）→ 撒播开 → 分散
info.example: The crowd began to disperse after the concert ended.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ place（位置）→ 使离开位置 → 移置
info.example: The new technology may displace many traditional workers.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ miss（送走）→ 送走 → 解雇
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so don't dismiss his potential.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ play（展开）→ 展开给人看 → 展示
info.example: The museum will display ancient artifacts next week.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ tort（扭转）→ 扭转开 → 扭曲
info.example: The mirror can distort your reflection.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ tract（拉）→ 把注意力拉开 → 分心
info.example: Please don't distract me while I'm studying.

info.memory_trick: dis（分离）+ tribute（给予）→ 分开给予 → 分配
info.example: The charity will distribute food to the homeless.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语介词 per，意为“通过、贯穿、完全”，在英语中作为前缀表示“贯穿、彻底、完全”或“通过某一过程”。

info.memory_trick: per 就是词根本身，记住“通过每个”即可。
info.example: He earns $50 per hour.

info.memory_trick: per（每）+ cent（一百）= 每一百中的数量，即百分比。
info.example: Only 20 percent of the students passed the exam.

info.memory_trick: percent + age（名词后缀），表示百分比的概念或数值。
info.example: The percentage of women in the workforce has increased.

info.memory_trick: per（完全）+ ceive（拿取）= 完全拿取信息，即感知或理解。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to perceive subtle changes in people's moods.

info.memory_trick: per（完全）+ fect（做）= 做得完全彻底，即完美的。
info.example: She spent years perfecting her craft.

info.memory_trick: oper（工作）+ ator（人）= 工作的人，即操作员。
info.example: The operator must follow all safety protocols.

info.memory_trick: oper（工作）+ ate（动词后缀）= 进行工作，即操作或运转。
info.example: He knows how to operate the machine.

info.memory_trick: oper（工作）+ ation（名词后缀）= 工作的过程或结果，即操作。
info.example: The operation was a success.

info.definition: 操作的；运营的；可使用的
info.memory_trick: operation + al（形容词后缀）= 与操作有关的。
info.example: The system is now fully operational.

info.memory_trick: op（朝向）+ port（港口）+ unity（状态）= 朝向港口的状态，即有利时机。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to seize every opportunity.

info.memory_trick: op（相反）+ pos（放置）+ ite（形容词后缀）= 放在对面的，即相反的。
info.example: They sat on opposite sides of the table.

info.memory_trick: op（相反）+ pose（放置）= 放在对立位置，即反对。
info.example: Many citizens oppose the new policy.

info.memory_trick: op（相反）+ pon（放置）+ ent（人）= 站在对面的人，即对手。
info.example: He defeated his opponent in the final match.

info.memory_trick: op（加强）+ press（压）= 用力压，即压迫。
info.example: The regime oppressed its citizens for decades.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 par（平等）和 videre（观看）的变体组合，后衍生出'观看'与'平等'双重含义。词根 par 强调对等、相同，peer 则侧重从旁观角度观察或地位相当者。

info.definition: n. 同辈，同龄人；贵族；v. 凝视，仔细看
info.memory_trick: 平等(par)的观看(peer)——同辈之间相互凝视，地位相当。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, often surprising his peers.

info.memory_trick: 使平等(par)——削去多余部分让事物变得整齐对等，如削苹果皮。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to pare down complex ideas into simple explanations.

info.memory_trick: 共同(com-)放在平等(par)的位置——放在一起比较异同。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to compare different language structures and find patterns.

info.memory_trick: 向(ad-)观看(peer)——使某物被看到，即出现。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to appear confident even in challenging situations.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'pecu'，原指‘牛群’，在古罗马时代牛群是重要的私人财产和财富象征，因此词根引申为‘私人财产’、‘金钱’相关的含义。

info.definition: 奇怪的，独特的；私人的，特有的
info.memory_trick: pecu（私人财产）+ liar（说谎者）→ 私人财产被说谎者盯上，情况变得‘奇怪’而‘独特’。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite peculiar this time of year.

info.memory_trick: pecu（私人财产）+ niary（联想‘货币’）→ 私人财产和货币相关，即‘金钱的’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he faces pecuniary difficulties in pursuing his studies.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语形容词magnus（大的）的最高级形式maximus（最大的），后经法语进入英语，核心含义为“极大、最高”，常与表示“程度、数量”的词根结合，衍生出大量与“极限、最大化”相关的词汇。

info.definition: n. 最大量，最大限度；adj. 最大的，最高的
info.memory_trick: max（最大）+ imum（拉丁语最高级后缀）→ 最大值。想象一个温度计上升到顶端刻度“MAX”，就是maximum。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: v. 使最大化，充分利用
info.memory_trick: max（最大）+ imize（动词后缀，表示“使成为”）→ 使达到最大。联想：把音量按钮调到“MAX”档，就是maximize音量。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

nodes.name: pay, pac (和平, 安抚, 支付)

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'pacare'（使和平、安抚）和 'pax'（和平），引申为通过支付达成和解或平息纷争。词根 'pay' 直接表示支付行为，而 'pac' 则强调和平与安抚的原始含义。

info.memory_trick: 想象你付钱（pay）时，对方露出和平（pac）的微笑，因为钱能安抚一切。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he must pay for the courses.

info.memory_trick: 支付（pay）+ 名词后缀-ment = 付款的行为或金额，记住：每次支付都是对和平（pac）的承诺。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he made the payment for the online course.

info.memory_trick: 发音类似 'piece'，想象一片（piece）宁静的和平（peace）之地，没有纷争。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which brings him inner peace.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'medius'（中间），后演变为古法语 'moyen'，进入英语后衍生出‘中间’、‘手段’和‘意味’等含义。核心概念是‘处于中间状态’或‘作为中介’，进而引申为‘表达某种意图或含义’。

info.definition: v. 意思是；意味着；打算；adj. 吝啬的；平均的；n. 平均数；中间
info.memory_trick: 联想‘中间’（mean）位置的人往往‘意味着’（mean）某种立场，或者‘平均’（mean）值在中间。
info.example: What does this word mean? (这个词是什么意思？)

info.memory_trick: mean（意味）+ ing（名词后缀）→ 意味的结果就是‘意义’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the meaning of this sentence is clear. (他快速学习新语言的能力非凡，但这句话的意思是清楚的。)

info.definition: n. 其间；同时；adv. 与此同时
info.memory_trick: mean（中间）+ time（时间）→ 两个事件之间的时间就是‘其间’。
info.example: We must abolish the outdated regulations; in the meantime, we should prepare new ones. (我们必须废除过时的法规；与此同时，我们应该准备新的。)

info.definition: adv. 与此同时；在此期间
info.memory_trick: mean（中间）+ while（时间段）→ 在中间的这段时间里→ ‘与此同时’。
info.example: He studied hard; meanwhile, his brother played video games. (他努力学习；与此同时，他的兄弟在玩电子游戏。)

info.definition: n. 方法；手段；财产；收入
info.memory_trick: mean（中间）+ s（复数）→ 实现目标的‘中间途径’就是‘方法、手段’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he lacks the means to travel abroad. (他快速学习新语言的能力非凡，但他缺少出国旅行的财力。)

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 'valere'（意为强健、有价值），后经古英语 'weorþ' 演变而来，核心含义为‘具有价值、值得’；词根强调事物本身的内在价值或应得的回报。

info.memory_trick: 联想‘worth = 我(w) + 地球(earth)’，地球是宝贵的，所以‘worth’就是‘值得’、‘有价值’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his effort is worth the time.

info.memory_trick: 拆分记忆：worth（价值）+ while（一段时间）→ 花时间有价值 → 值得做的。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making every study session worthwhile.

info.definition: 值得的；应得的；有价值的
info.memory_trick: 联想‘worthy = worth + y（形容词后缀）’，直接记忆为‘有价值的、值得的’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he is worthy of praise.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'scribere'，意为 '写、刻写'，其过去分词形式 'scriptus' 衍生出 'script'。该词根广泛用于英语中表示与书写、记录相关的词汇，强调用手或工具在表面上留下痕迹的动作。

info.memory_trick: 想象用手（right hand）在纸上写（write）字，发音同 'right'，但拼写不同，记住 'w' 像笔尖。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often writes down new words to remember them.

info.memory_trick: write + er（表示人），即 '写字的人'，联想到职业作家。
info.example: The writer spent years perfecting his novel, showing a remarkable ability to learn new writing techniques quickly.

info.definition: 脚本；手稿；笔迹；文字系统
info.memory_trick: 来自 'scrib' 的过去分词，想象剧本（script）是写好的文字，演员照着念。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he can even read scripts in those languages.

info.memory_trick: de（向下）+ scribe（写），意为 '写下来'，引申为用语言详细描绘。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he can describe complex ideas with ease.

info.origin: 源自希腊语 'skopein'，意为'看、观察、检查'，后经拉丁语进入英语，常用于构成与观察、视觉或检查相关的词汇。

info.definition: n. 范围；视野；机会；v. 仔细看，审视
info.memory_trick: scop（看）+ e → 眼睛能看到的地方就是“范围”。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which broadens the scope of his career opportunities.

info.memory_trick: micro（小）+ scope（看）→ 用来观察微小物体的仪器。
info.example: Under the microscope, the scientist observed tiny organisms that were invisible to the naked eye.

info.memory_trick: tele（远）+ scope（看）→ 用来观察远处物体的仪器。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, much like a telescope allows one to see distant stars clearly.

info.origin: 源自原始印欧语词根 *h₃reǵ-，意为‘伸直、引导、排列’。在拉丁语中演变为 ringere（排列成行）和 ordinare（安排顺序），最终通过法语进入英语，核心含义为‘按顺序放置’或‘划分等级’。该词根强调‘有序的线性排列’，并延伸出‘级别’和‘范围’等抽象概念。

info.definition: n. 等级，军衔；排，行；v. 排列，分等级；adj. 茂密的，难闻的
info.memory_trick: 想象军队列队（rank）时，按照军衔（rank）高低排列，高官站在前排。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which ranks him among the top linguists in the field.

info.definition: n. 范围，幅度；山脉；v. 排列，（在一定范围内）变化
info.memory_trick: 山脉（range）像一排排（rank）连绵起伏的线条，延伸出广阔的范围（range）。
info.example: The mountains range from 1,000 to 3,000 meters in height, offering a wide range of climbing challenges.

info.memory_trick: 前缀ar-（加强）+ range（排列）→ 把东西排整齐，就是‘安排’好一切。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he arranged his study schedule to practice three languages daily.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 'urgere'，意为‘驱使、催促、紧逼’。该词根强调一种内在或外在的推动力，促使行动或反应发生，常带有紧迫或压力感。

info.memory_trick: urg（催促）+ ent（形容词后缀）→ 需要催促的 → 紧急的。想象一个不断催促你完成的任务，就是urgent。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but this urgent deadline requires immediate action.

info.definition: 强烈欲望；冲动；催促；驱策
info.memory_trick: 直接来自词根urg，意为‘驱使’→ 内心被驱使的感觉 → 强烈冲动。想象一股力量推着你去做某事。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he felt a strong urge to master another one.

info.origin: 词根through/thr源自原始日耳曼语*thurkh，与古英语þurh（穿过）同源，核心含义为“从一端到另一端”或“完全地”。引申为“贯穿、彻底”，常表示动作的完成、空间的穿越或状态的持续。

info.definition: prep. 穿过，通过；adv. 彻底地，自始至终
info.memory_trick: 想象一根针穿过（through）布料，thr-就像针尖的穿透动作。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he works through every challenge with determination.

info.definition: prep. 遍及，贯穿；adv. 到处，始终
info.memory_trick: through（穿过）+ out（出去）= 从一头穿到另一头再出去，即“遍及整个范围”。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his enthusiasm remains throughout the entire course.

info.definition: v. 茁壮成长，兴旺发达
info.memory_trick: thr-（穿过）+ -ive（动词后缀）= 生命之力穿透阻碍，蓬勃生长。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he thrives in multilingual environments.

info.definition: n. 激动，震颤；v. 使兴奋，使震颤
info.memory_trick: thr-（穿过）+ -ill（像ill疾病）= 一种穿透身体的强烈感觉，既可能是激动也可能是恐惧。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and the thrill of mastering a new dialect keeps him motivated.

info.memory_trick: thr-（穿过）+ -ift（如gift礼物）= 让每一分钱都“穿过”最有效的用途，不浪费。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his thrift allows him to fund his language courses.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 pater（父亲），引申为‘父亲般的’、‘典范’、‘模式’等含义。词根 patr- 表示‘父亲’，而 pattern 则演变自同一根源，指代可模仿的榜样或模板。

info.memory_trick: 想象父亲（patr）是孩子模仿的‘模式’和‘典范’，他的一举一动都成为可复制的‘图案’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, following a pattern of consistent practice.

info.memory_trick: 父亲（patr）常是家庭的经济支持者，就像赞助人（patron）资助艺术家或老主顾照顾生意。
info.example: The wealthy patron sponsored the artist's exhibition.

info.memory_trick: 父亲（patr）发明了独特的方法，申请‘专利’（patent）来保护自己的成果，就像保护自己的‘模式’不被抄袭。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a patent advantage in his career.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'mirus'（惊奇），后经古法语 'merveille' 进入英语，核心含义为‘令人惊叹的事物或情感’，强调超出常规、引发敬畏或好奇的心理状态。

info.definition: 惊奇，奇迹；感到惊讶，想知道
info.memory_trick: 联想‘wander’（漫游）——在漫游中看到奇景就会‘wonder’（惊叹）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: wonder（奇迹）+ ful（充满）→ 充满奇迹的 → 极好的。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'opera'（工作、劳动、努力）和 'opus'（作品、成果），核心含义为通过劳动创造出的产物。在英语中，这两个词根衍生出与操作、作品、艺术创作相关的词汇，强调从努力到成果的转化过程。

info.memory_trick: 想象一部歌剧是许多音乐家合作的‘作品’（opus），需要大量‘工作’（opera）排练。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, just as a conductor learns an opera score.

info.memory_trick: 词根'oper'（工作）+ 'ate'（动词后缀）→ 做工作 → 操作。想象医生‘工作’在手术台上。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him operate effectively in international teams.

info.memory_trick: 来自'operate'（操作）+ 'tion'（名词后缀）→ 操作的过程或结果。联想一次精密的‘手术’需要团队协作的‘工作’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making him invaluable for complex operations abroad.

info.memory_trick: 'operate'（操作）+ 'or'（人）→ 执行操作的人。想象电话‘接线员’在‘工作’中快速切换线路。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which would make him an excellent operator in a multilingual call center.

info.definition: 操作的；运营的；可使用的
info.memory_trick: 'operation'（操作）+ 'al'（形容词后缀）→ 与操作相关的。联想一个‘运营’团队不断‘工作’使系统保持可用状态。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, ensuring the team remains operational in diverse regions.

info.memory_trick: 'master'（大师）+ 'piece'（作品）→ 大师的作品。词根'opus'（作品）隐含其中，指通过精湛技艺完成的伟大‘工作’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a skill that could help him create a linguistic masterpiece.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'mater'（母亲），后引申为 '同伴、配偶'，词根 'mate' 表示 '共同生活或合作的人'，也指 '匹配、结合' 的概念。

info.memory_trick: 想象你和最好的朋友一起下棋，你们是棋友（mate），互相配合。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his study mate often benefits from his help.

info.definition: 材料；原料；物质的；重要的
info.memory_trick: 材料（material）是制作物品的'同伴'，没有它，任何东西都无法成形。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often uses various learning materials to practice.

info.definition: 成熟的；到期的；充分发展的
info.memory_trick: 成熟的（mature）果实就像找到了'同伴'——时间与养分，共同成就了饱满的状态。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, showing a mature understanding of grammar rules.

info.memory_trick: 数学（mathematical）需要数字和公式像'同伴'一样紧密协作，才能得出精确结果。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, approaching patterns with a mathematical precision.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'securus'（安全的），由 'se-'（无）和 'cura'（忧虑、关心）组合而成，字面意为“无忧无虑”。词根 'cur' 本身也表示“关心、注意”，因此在英语中 'secure' 和 'cure' 都隐含了从“关心”到“使安全”或“治愈”的语义延伸。

info.definition: adj. 安全的；牢固的；有把握的 v. 使安全；获得；固定
info.memory_trick: 谐音“西克尤尔”，联想成“西边克服了忧虑”，从而感到安全。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes his future career very secure.

info.definition: n. 安全；安保；保障；抵押品
info.memory_trick: secure（安全）+ ity（名词后缀）→ 安全的状态或系统。想象一个“安全门”上写着“security”。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which gives him a sense of job security.

info.definition: v. 治愈；治疗；解决 n. 疗法；药物
info.memory_trick: 谐音“可忧”，但实际是“消除忧虑”——治愈疾病让人不再担忧。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but there is no quick cure for a broken heart.

info.origin: 词根 tip/top 源自原始印欧语 *teup-，意为‘突出、尖端’。该词根在英语中演化为表示‘顶端、最高点’的概念，并引申出‘提示、小费’等含义（因‘尖端’可指‘细微的指点’）。其核心意象是物体的最高或最尖处，常与‘顶点、极致’相关。

info.memory_trick: 想象一个‘尖’（tip）的铅笔头，轻轻一‘点’（tip）就能给出‘提示’（tip）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he always shares useful tips with his classmates.

info.memory_trick: ‘Top’像‘塔顶’的‘顶’（top），想象一座塔的‘顶部’（top）是最高点。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he always ranks at the top of his class.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'scortum'（皮、割下的皮）及 'scindere'（切割）的变体，核心含义为‘切割、刻划’。词根通过物理切割动作引申出‘刻痕、分数（如比赛中的记分）’以及‘轻蔑（如划痕般的贬低）’等抽象义。

info.definition: n. 得分；分数；刻痕；乐谱 v. 得分；刻划；记分
info.memory_trick: 想象在木板上用刀刻下一道道痕迹（score），每刻一道代表得一分，如同比赛记分板上的刻痕。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, scoring high on every test.

info.definition: n. 轻蔑；鄙视 v. 轻蔑；不屑（做某事）
info.memory_trick: 用刀（sc-）在心上划出刻痕（-orn），表示对某人或某事的极度鄙视，如同在心上留下轻蔑的伤疤。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he scorns those who struggle with even basic phrases.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'utilis'，意为‘有用的、可用的’，进一步追溯至 'uti'（使用、利用）。该词根强调‘能够被使用或产生效益’的概念，是英语中表达功能性、实用性的核心词根。

info.definition: 利用；使用（尤指为实际目的而有效使用）
info.memory_trick: util（使用）+ ize（动词后缀）→ 使有用 → 利用。联想：把闲置资源‘实用化’（utilize）就是让它变得有用。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he knows how to utilize every resource available to him.

info.definition: 利用；使用（强调过程或状态）
info.memory_trick: util（使用）+ isation（名词后缀）→ 使用的过程。记忆：‘利用’（utilisation）就是让东西‘有用起来’的动作。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so the utilisation of proper gear is essential for safety.

info.memory_trick: util（有用）+ ity（名词后缀）→ 有用性。联想：一个东西的‘效用’（utility）就是它‘有多有用’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is of great utility in his international career.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语‘magister’，意为‘首领、教师、有权威的人’，后经古法语‘maistre’进入英语，核心含义为‘掌控、精通’的人或状态。

info.definition: 主人；大师；硕士；精通，掌握
info.memory_trick: 想象一位‘大师’（master）在‘掌控’（master）全局，就像魔术师挥动魔杖。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and aims to master three more this year.

info.memory_trick: ‘大师’（master）的‘作品’（piece）就是‘杰作’（masterpiece），好比达芬奇的《蒙娜丽莎》。
info.example: The painting is considered a masterpiece of Renaissance art.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'regula'（直尺、规则），后经古法语 'reille'（铁轨、横杆）进入英语。核心含义为‘笔直的条状物’，引申为‘轨道’和‘栏杆’，强调其引导、限制或支撑的功能。

info.definition: n. 轨道；栏杆；铁轨 v. 抱怨；责骂
info.memory_trick: 想象火车在笔直的‘轨道’上行驶，你扶着‘栏杆’站稳，同时有人因火车晚点而‘抱怨’（rail against）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: n. 铁路；铁道部门 v. 迫使…仓促行事；草率通过
info.memory_trick: rail（轨道）+ road（路）= 用铁轨铺成的路，即‘铁路’。联想：铁路公司常‘迫使’立法机构快速通过法案。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

info.definition: n. 铁路；铁道（英式英语常用）
info.memory_trick: rail（轨道）+ way（道路）= 轨道之路，即‘铁路’。与railroad同义，但railway更强调轨道系统本身。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: v. 召集；重整；恢复 n. 集会；拉力赛
info.memory_trick: 词根rail（轨道）变形为rally，想象人们沿着‘轨道’（路线）‘集合’起来，或赛车在赛道上‘恢复’速度。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'pannus'（布片、碎布），后通过古法语 'pieche'（片、块）进入英语，引申为‘补丁’、‘小块区域’或‘修补’。在现代英语中，patch 还衍生出‘临时修复’或‘一小片’的概念。

info.definition: n. 补丁；小块；眼罩；斑点；v. 修补；打补丁
info.memory_trick: 想象一块布片（patch）盖在衣服破洞上，就像给衣服打了一个‘补丁’；或联想到电脑‘补丁’程序，用来修复漏洞。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still needs to patch the gaps in his grammar.

info.definition: v. 派遣；发送；迅速处理；n. 急件；派遣；迅速处理
info.memory_trick: 前缀 dis-（分开、离开）+ patch（片、补丁）→ 把‘片’（消息或任务）‘分开送走’→ 派遣、发送。想象将一封封‘急件’像补丁一样快速‘发送’出去。
info.example: The manager had to dispatch the urgent orders with great speed, much like his ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'titulus'，意为‘铭文、标签、荣誉头衔’，引申为‘标题、称号’，核心含义是‘标记身份或内容的名称’。

info.definition: 标题；头衔；称号；所有权凭证
info.memory_trick: 联想‘tie它’——用标题把文章内容‘系’在一起，或头衔把身份‘系’住。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: 给……定名；使有资格；给……称号
info.memory_trick: en-（使）+ title（标题/权利）→ 使拥有标题或权利，即‘定名’或‘使有资格’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'urbs'，意为‘城市’、‘城邦’。在古罗马时期，urbs特指罗马城本身，后来泛指一切城市。该词根强调城市的政治、文化中心属性，与‘城市文明’、‘城市化’等概念紧密相关。

info.definition: adj. 城市的，都市的；住在都市的
info.memory_trick: urb（城市）+ an（形容词后缀）→ 城市的。想象‘城市里的公交（urban bus）’帮助记忆。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is essential in an urban environment with diverse cultures.

info.definition: n. 郊区，城郊；近郊住宅区
info.memory_trick: sub（在…之下，靠近）+ urb（城市）→ 靠近城市的地方 → 郊区。想象‘地铁（subway）从城市开往郊区（suburb）’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helped him adapt to life in the suburb where many immigrants settled.

info.origin: 源自原始印欧语词根 *sekʷ-，意为‘看、跟随、寻找’。该词根在拉丁语中演变为 sequi（跟随），在古英语中演变为 sēon（看）和 sēcan（寻找），并进一步衍生出 seem（看起来），核心语义为‘通过视觉或感知去发现或追随目标’。

info.memory_trick: 想象‘see’就像用眼睛‘C’形扫描，看到一切。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and I see great potential in him.

info.memory_trick: ‘seek’和‘see’相似，但多了‘k’，像‘看’到目标后‘刻’意去追。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he will seek every opportunity to practice.

info.memory_trick: ‘seem’像‘see’加‘m’，‘看’到模糊的‘模样’，所以是‘似乎’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which may seem effortless to others.

info.memory_trick: ‘seem’加‘ingly’，像‘似乎’的副词形式，表示‘表面上看’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, seemingly without any struggle.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'randon'，意为'猛烈的奔跑'或'无目的冲撞'；后进入古法语 'randir'（奔跑、疾驰），最终在英语中演变为'无规律、偶然的'含义。词根核心意象为'不受控制的快速移动'，引申为'随机、任意'。

info.definition: adj. 随机的，任意的；n. 随机；随意
info.memory_trick: 联想'run'（奔跑）+ 'dom'（领域）→ 在领域内乱跑，毫无规律 → 随机
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his study schedule is completely random.

info.definition: n. 随机性，偶然性；无规律的状态
info.memory_trick: random（随机）+ ness（名词后缀）→ 随机性；也可以记作'乱（ran）动（dom）的状态'
info.example: The randomness of the data made it difficult to identify any pattern.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语‘utter’（外部、彻底），引申为‘完全的、彻底的’或‘说出、发声’，强调从内部到外部的表达或状态的极致。

info.definition: 完全的，彻底的；发出声音，说出
info.memory_trick: 联想‘out-er’（向外），完全从内向外表达或彻底显现。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is an utter surprise to everyone.

info.memory_trick: utter + ly = 副词形式，表示‘完全地’，如‘utterly impossible’（完全不可能）。
info.example: He was utterly convinced that he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'vertere' 的变体，通过古英语 'weorðscipe'（价值+状态）演变而来，核心语义为‘赋予价值、敬仰’，后引申为对神或崇高事物的崇拜行为。

info.memory_trick: 联想 'worth'（价值）+ 'ship'（身份/状态），认为某物有极高价值而给予敬仰身份。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he never worships his own talent.

info.memory_trick: 与 'worship' 同源，去掉 'ship' 保留核心‘价值’，记住‘有 worth 才值得 worship’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and that skill is worth a lot in the global market.

info.origin: 源自原始印欧语根 *h₁er-（升起、移动），通过拉丁语 *ras-（抬起）和古诺尔斯语 reisa（举起）演变而来，核心含义为“从低处向高处移动”或“使处于更高或更显著的位置”，引申出“提高、增加、唤醒、召集”等抽象意义。

info.definition: 举起；提高；增加；筹集；引起
info.memory_trick: 想象一个人举起（raise）双手，手掌向上托举，动作干脆有力，就像在说“raise”时嘴巴张大向上提拉。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which raises his confidence in international meetings.

info.definition: 上升；升起；增加；起床；起义
info.memory_trick: 太阳每天自动升起（rise），无需外力，记住“rise”是不及物动词，自己往上走，字母‘i’像一个人站立挺直。
info.example: The sun will rise at 6:30 tomorrow morning, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, waking up early to study.

info.definition: 召集；集合；恢复；重新振作；汽车拉力赛
info.memory_trick: 想象一群人被召集（rally）在一起，大家举起手（raise）欢呼，场面热闹，rally = raise + ally（盟友），盟友一起举起手。
info.example: Despite the setback, the team managed to rally and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helped them communicate with international partners.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'medianus'，意为‘中间的’，后演变为古法语 'meien'，进入英语后引申出‘中间状态’、‘平均’、‘手段’等含义。核心概念是‘处于两端之间的位置或数值’。

info.definition: v. 意思是；打算；导致 adj. 吝啬的；平均的；中间的 n. 平均值；中间
info.memory_trick: 想象一个天平：中间指针指在‘平均’刻度上，两边分别放着‘吝啬’和‘慷慨’的砝码——指针就是 mean。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly. (此句不含mean，但可替换为：What does this word mean in this context?)

info.definition: n. 方法；手段；收入（复数形式常作单数用）
info.memory_trick: ‘mean’加上‘s’变成复数，就像‘方法’不止一种——中间有多种途径达到目标。
info.example: We must abolish the outdated regulations. (此句不含means，可替换为：We must find effective means to solve this problem.)

info.definition: n. 其间；同时 adv. 与此同时
info.memory_trick: ‘mean’（中间）+‘time’（时间）→ 两段事件之间的时间，即‘在此期间’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly; in the meantime, he also masters several dialects.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语前缀 ad-，表示“朝向、向、接近”，在英语中常因后续辅音同化而变为 ac-, af-, ag-, al-, ap-, ar-, as-, at- 等形式。核心含义是“向某方向移动或靠近”。

info.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cess（走）+ ory（名词后缀）→ 跟着主要物品走的东西 → 附件
info.example: He bought several car accessories, including a phone holder and a dash cam.

info.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cept（拿）+ ance（名词后缀）→ 伸手去拿 → 接受
info.example: His acceptance of the award was met with thunderous applause.

info.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cept（拿）→ 伸手去拿 → 接受
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ cess（走）→ 走向某处 → 进入
info.example: Students have free access to the library's online resources.

info.memory_trick: ac-（朝向）+ celer（快）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使朝向快 → 加速
info.example: The government plans to accelerate the construction of new highways.

info.origin: 词根field源自古英语feld，意为开阔的土地、田野，引申为领域、范围、场地等。其核心含义是‘一片开阔的空间或区域’，常用来表示知识、活动或工作的特定领域。

info.definition: n. 田，地；牧场；（知识或活动的）领域，范围；场地，场所；v. 接（球），接防；处理，应付（问题或意见）
info.memory_trick: 想象一片开阔的田野（field），你可以在这里自由奔跑，就像在某个知识领域（field）里自由探索。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which gives him an advantage in the field of linguistics.

info.origin: 词根fer-源自拉丁语ferus，意为‘野生的、未驯服的’，引申为‘激烈的、狂暴的’。在英语中，fer-直接体现了原始未驯化的野性力量，常用来描述情感或行为的强烈与不可控。

info.definition: adj. 激烈的；凶猛的；强烈的；狂热的
info.memory_trick: 想象一头野生的狮子（fer-）在咆哮，它的眼神‘fierce’（凶猛）得让人不敢靠近。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词'cambiare'，意为'交换、改变'，后经由古法语进入英语，核心语义围绕'转变、替换、交替'展开，引申出机会、渠道、挑战等概念。

info.memory_trick: cambi- 改变 → change 发音类似'趁之'，趁势改变自己。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: 改变(cambi)带来机会(chance)：人生改变往往源于一次偶然的机遇。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: 改变(cambi)的通道(channel)：要改变现状，需要找到正确的渠道。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: 改变(cambi)带来挑战(challenge)：每一次改变都伴随着新的挑战。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自原始日耳曼语 *thraut-（压迫、折磨），古英语中作 þrēat（压迫、威胁），与拉丁语 trudere（推挤）同源。核心含义为‘施加压力或危险’，后引申为‘预示伤害的迹象或言语’。

info.memory_trick: threat = th（think思考）+reat（great大）→ 想到一个大坏蛋在威胁你
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his reckless behavior poses a threat to the team's safety.

info.definition: v. 威胁；恐吓；预示危险
info.memory_trick: threat（威胁）+ en（使动词后缀）→ 使…受到威胁，即‘威胁’的动作
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his constant lateness threatens to undermine the project's deadline.

info.origin: 词根gin-源自拉丁语动词gignere（意为“产生、生育、开始”），其过去分词genitus和现在分词gignens衍生出众多与“起源、生成”相关的词汇。在英语中，gin-通过古法语进入，核心含义为“开始、启动”，强调动作的初始阶段。与同源词根gen-（如generate）相比，gin-更侧重“发起”这一瞬间动作。

info.memory_trick: 联想：be（是）+ gin（开始）→ “就是开始”，直接对应词根含义。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he will begin studying Japanese next month.

info.memory_trick: begin + ning（动名词后缀）→ 将“开始”名词化，意为“开始的状态或时间点”。
info.example: The beginning of his language learning journey was challenging, but he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语词根 'bell-'（原意为‘力量、意愿’），后引申为‘使信服、信任’。在英语中，bel- 常出现在与‘相信、信仰’相关的词汇中，强调内心对某事物真实性的认可。

info.memory_trick: bel-（相信）+ -ief（名词后缀）→ 相信的状态或事物。可以联想‘b’像一个人张开双臂拥抱真理，'elief'谐音‘一来福’→ 相信能带来福气。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: bel-（相信）+ -ieve（动词后缀）→ 去相信。可以拆解为‘be + lie + ve’：be（是）+ lie（谎言）→ 即使听起来像谎言，也选择‘是’（相信），ve代表‘验证’后依然相信。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自印欧语系词根 *der-（拉、拖），在拉丁语中演变为 trahere（拉），后进入古英语和法语，最终以 draw 形式进入现代英语，核心含义为“通过拉力使物体移动或分离”。

info.memory_trick: with（向后）+ draw（拉）→ 向后拉 → 撤回、退出
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he decided to withdraw from the advanced class to focus on basics.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'clinare'，意为'倾斜、弯曲、偏向'。该词根引申出物理上的倾斜、趋势上的倾向以及心理上的偏好等含义。

info.definition: v. 倾斜；倾向（于）；n. 斜面，斜坡
info.memory_trick: in-（进入）+ clin-（倾斜）= 向里倾斜，即倾向于某方向
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes me incline to believe he is a linguistic genius.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 globus，意为‘球、球体、团块’，引申为‘球形、全球、整体’的概念。该词根强调‘球形’或‘完整的一体’，在英语中常用来描述与地球、球体或整体性相关的词汇。

info.memory_trick: glob（球）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 像球一样覆盖整个地球 → 全球的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a global asset in today's interconnected world.

info.memory_trick: glob（球）+ e（名词后缀）→ 直接指球体或地球
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he dreams of traveling around the globe to use them.

info.origin: 源自原始印欧语词根 *weid-（看见、知道），拉丁语中演变为 vidēre（看见），在日耳曼语系中通过音变形成 wit-，核心含义为“通过视觉获得知识”，引申为“知道、见证、智慧”。

info.definition: n. 目击者，证人；v. 目击，见证
info.memory_trick: wit（知道）+ ness（名词后缀）→ 亲眼“知道”事件的人 → 目击者
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as witnessed by his fluency in five languages.

info.definition: n. 智慧，才智；机智，风趣
info.memory_trick: 直接来自词根 wit-，知道得多自然有“智慧”，反应快则显“机智”
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a wit that impresses everyone.

info.origin: 词根first-源自古英语‘fyrst’，意为‘最前面的、最初的’，与原始日耳曼语‘furistaz’同源，强调时间或顺序上的领先地位。在英语中常作为形容词或名词核心，表示‘第一’或‘首要’。

info.definition: 第一的；最初的；首要的；最先
info.memory_trick: 想象‘first’就像‘fast’（快速）的变体——跑得最快的人就是第一名。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'longus'，本义为‘长’；在古法语中引申出‘属于’的含义，表示‘长久地属于某物或某人’，后进入英语演变为‘belong’。

info.definition: v. 属于；应被放置；是……的成员
info.memory_trick: be（是）+ long（长）→ 长久地是某物的一部分 → 属于
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'liber'，意为自由、不受束缚。引申为权衡、斟酌，因为在自由状态下人们会仔细衡量各种选择。

info.definition: adj. 故意的；深思熟虑的；从容的。v. 仔细考虑；商议。
info.memory_trick: de（加强）+ liber（权衡）+ ate（动词/形容词后缀）= 反复权衡，即深思熟虑；故意为之也是经过权衡的结果。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 facere（做、使）的过去分词 factus，后经古法语进入英语，演变为 fit，核心含义为“使合适、符合、适应”，强调事物之间的匹配或适应关系。

info.definition: v. 适合，合身；adj. 合适的，健康的
info.memory_trick: 想象一件衣服（fit）正好合身，就像拼图（fit）完美匹配。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but this method doesn't fit his learning style.

info.definition: adj. 适合的，恰当的；n. 配件，试穿
info.memory_trick: fit（适合）+ ing（进行时）→ 正在使东西合适的过程，比如试穿衣服或安装配件。
info.example: It is fitting that he should receive the award for his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自原始印欧语根 *wen-（意为“渴望、追求、努力获得”），后进入拉丁语和日耳曼语族，演变为表示“通过努力或竞争而获得胜利”的含义。在英语中直接体现为动词 win，强调通过奋斗或优势地位取得成果。

info.definition: 赢得；获胜；在竞争、比赛或努力中取得胜利或获得某物
info.memory_trick: 想象你在比赛中挥动拳头（win 的发音像“win”），大声喊“我赢了！”——每次看到 win 就联想到胜利的欢呼。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自原始日耳曼语 *upp-，意为'向上、在上'，与古英语 uppan（向上）同源。在英语构词中，up- 常作为前缀表示空间上的向上移动，或引申为状态提升、更新、完成等抽象含义。

info.definition: 更新；使现代化；向…提供最新信息
info.memory_trick: up（向上）+ date（日期）→ 把日期向上推 → 更新到最新状态
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to update his knowledge quickly.

info.definition: 升级；提升；提高（质量、等级等）
info.memory_trick: up（向上）+ grade（等级）→ 等级向上提升 → 升级
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so hikers should upgrade their gear.

info.definition: 最新的；现代的；包含最新信息的
info.memory_trick: up（向上）+ to（到达）+ date（日期）→ 向上到达最新日期 → 紧跟时代
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to stay up-to-date with new languages.

info.definition: 使心烦意乱；打乱；推翻；不适的
info.memory_trick: up（向上）+ set（放置）→ 原本放好的东西被向上翻倒 → 打乱秩序，引申为心烦
info.example: The sudden change in weather upset their hiking plans.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'deliberare'，由 de-（完全、离开）和 liberare（释放、使自由）组成，原意为“完全释放”，后引申为“交付、递送、给予”。在英语中核心含义是“将某物从一方转移至另一方”，涵盖物品、服务、言论（如演讲）或结果（如成果）的传递。

info.definition: n. 交付；递送；投递；分娩；演讲方式
info.memory_trick: 联想：de（向下）+ liver（肝脏）+ y → 想象医生将“肝脏”安全“向下”取出并“交付”给患者，强调传递和最终结果。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: v. 交付；递送；发表（演讲）；接生；履行（承诺）
info.memory_trick: 拆解：de（加强）+ liver（肝脏）→ 想象快递员“加强”力度，把“肝脏”这种重要物品按时“递送”到目的地，强调动作本身。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 delicatus，意为‘精致的、美味的、令人愉悦的’，引申为‘精致’与‘诱惑’双重含义，强调感官上的细腻吸引或诱发性。

info.definition: adj. 精致的，脆弱的，微妙的；易碎的
info.memory_trick: 联想‘delic-’（精致）+‘ate’（吃）→ 需要小心品尝的精致食物，引申为任何需要谨慎对待的脆弱事物。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

info.definition: vt. 诱出，引出（反应、信息等）
info.memory_trick: e-（向外）+licit（来自delic-的变体，诱惑）→ 通过诱惑或巧妙引导使某物‘出来’，即诱出。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语词根'benē'，意为'好、善、有利'，常见于英语中表示正面意义的词汇，如benefit（利益）、benevolent（仁慈的）。

info.memory_trick: bene（好）+fic（做）+ial（形容词后缀）→ 做出好结果 → 有益的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is highly beneficial for his career.

info.memory_trick: bene（好）+fit（做）→ 做得好带来好处 → 利益
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and this benefit helps him collaborate with international teams.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'cantare'（唱）和 'incantare'（施咒/激励），本义为‘歌唱以鼓舞’，后引申为‘激励、刺激’。词根核心意象是‘用声音或言语激发行动’。

info.memory_trick: in（进入）+ cent（唱/激励）+ ive → 进入激励状态的东西 → 激励、诱因。想象一首振奋人心的战歌让人充满动力。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the promise of a bonus was the main incentive for him to study even harder.

info.origin: 源自希腊语 'embryon'，意为‘在体内生长之物’，由 en-（在…内）和 bryein（成长、膨胀）组成，引申为胚胎、初期发育阶段。词根强调生命的初始形态和未成熟的发育过程。

info.definition: n. 胚胎；初期，萌芽阶段
info.memory_trick: 联想记忆：em（进入）+ bryo（膨胀）→ 进入膨胀状态 → 胚胎在母体内逐渐膨胀发育。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as if his brain is still in its embryo stage of linguistic plasticity.

info.origin: 源自原始日耳曼语 *fehtaną，意为‘斗争、搏斗’，与古英语 feohtan 同源，后演变为现代英语 fight，核心语义是‘为达到目的而进行的身体或精神对抗’。

info.memory_trick: 想象‘飞(fai)踢(t)’的动作，就是一场战斗。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still had to fight with frustration when starting French.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'invenire'（找到、遇见），后经由古法语 'finden' 进入中古英语，核心含义为‘通过寻找或偶然获得’；词根 'find-' 强调‘发现’的动作与结果，常引申为‘领悟、查明’等认知过程。

info.definition: n. 发现；发现物；调查（或研究）的结果
info.memory_trick: find（发现）+ ing（名词后缀）→ 发现的东西或结果。想象你找到一把钥匙，这个‘finding’就是你的发现物。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'bracchium'，意为 '手臂'。引申为 '支撑、环抱' 等含义，因手臂常用来拥抱或支撑物体。

info.memory_trick: em-（进入）+ brace（手臂）→ 进入手臂中 → 拥抱；引申为 '接受'，如欣然接受一个想法。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, but we still embrace the adventure.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'degradus'，由 de-（向下）和 gradus（步伐、等级）组成，本义为‘向下走的一步’，引申为‘等级、程度、阶段’。在英语中，degree 既表示‘程度、等级’，也表示‘学位、度（温度/角度）’，核心概念是‘由低到高的阶梯或度量单位’。

info.definition: n. 程度；等级；学位；度（温度/角度）
info.memory_trick: 联想记忆：de（向下）+ gree（=grade等级）→ 一步步向下或向上走的等级，如温度计上的刻度、教育的学位阶梯。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自希腊语 'kharaktēr'，意为‘雕刻的工具’或‘刻印的标记’，后引申为‘独特的印记、特征’。在拉丁语中演变为 'character'，强调个体或事物区别于其他事物的本质标志。

info.definition: n. 特征，特点；adj. 典型的，特有的
info.memory_trick: 联想‘character’（特征）+‘istic’（形容词后缀），即‘具有特征的’；也可拆解为‘char’（茶）+‘act’（行动）+‘eristic’（争论的），想象‘喝茶时争论某人的典型特征’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a characteristic of his sharp mind.

info.definition: n. 性格，品质；角色；字符；特征
info.memory_trick: 拆解为‘char’（茶）+‘acter’（行动者），想象‘喝茶的人表现出独特的性格’；或联想‘car’（车）+‘character’（特征），每辆车都有其品牌特征。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a testament to his strong character and persistence.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 augurium，原指古罗马通过观察鸟类的飞行、叫声等自然现象来占卜神意的祭司行为，后引申为‘预兆’、‘开始’的含义。该词根隐含从征兆中推断未来、开启新进程的意象。

info.definition: 开始；开创；为…举行就职典礼（或开幕式）
info.memory_trick: 拆解为 in（进入）+ augur（预兆）+ ate（动词后缀），想象‘进入有预兆的时刻’，即举行仪式开启新阶段，如总统就职、大楼落成等。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which will surely inaugurate a new chapter in his career.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'implicare'（缠绕、包含），后经古法语 'embeder' 演变而来，核心含义为‘将某物牢固地置于另一物内部’，强调深度结合与不可分割性。

info.definition: 嵌入；把…牢牢地嵌入（或插入、埋入）；使深留于（意识、记忆等）
info.memory_trick: em-（进入）+ bed（床）→ 把东西放进床垫里，就像嵌入一样牢固。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, embedding vocabulary and grammar into his memory with ease.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'corpus'，意为身体、实体。在英语中引申为物质实体、整体或具体化，强调将抽象概念赋予物理形态或使之可见。

info.definition: 体现，使具体化；包含，收录
info.memory_trick: em-（进入）+ body（身体）→ 让抽象事物“进入身体”→ 使具体化、体现出来。想象一个抽象的想法变成了有血有肉的人。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which embodies the essence of linguistic talent.

info.origin: 源自原始日耳曼语 *thurstuz，与古英语 thurst（口渴）、古高地德语 durst 同源，核心含义为‘干燥引起的渴望’，后引申为对任何事物的强烈渴求。

info.definition: n. 渴，口渴；渴望，热望 v. 渴望；口渴
info.memory_trick: thirst → 想象在沙漠中行走，舌头干得发烫（th- 像舌头伸出，irst 像 first 口渴是第一需求）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his thirst for knowledge never fades.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'tenere'（持有、保持）的变体，在英语中演变为 'hold'，核心含义为‘举起、支撑、维持’，引申为‘支持、维护’等抽象概念。词根 'hold-' 直接继承此意，强调‘稳固地持有或举起’的动作或状态。

info.definition: 支持，维护（尤指原则、法律或决定）
info.memory_trick: up（向上）+ hold（举起）→ 向上举起，即‘支持、维护’某事物不倒。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'finis'，意为‘界限、终点、结束’。在英语中，词根 fin- 常引申为‘限定、终结、边界’的概念，并进一步衍生出‘明确、财务’等抽象含义。

info.memory_trick: de（加强）+ fin（界限）+ ition（名词后缀）→ 明确划定界限 → 定义
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is the very definition of a polyglot.

info.memory_trick: de（向下）+ fin（界限）→ 向下划界限 → 界定、定义
info.example: It is difficult to define the exact boundaries of this concept without more data.

info.memory_trick: fin（界限）+ ite（形容词后缀）→ 有界限的 → 有限的
info.example: The resources available for the project are finite, so we must prioritize.

info.memory_trick: de（加强）+ fin（界限）+ ite（形容词后缀）→ 界限清晰的 → 明确的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he needs a definite schedule to maintain progress.

info.memory_trick: fin（结束/界限）+ ancial（形容词后缀）→ 与金钱的最终结算相关 → 财务的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him in financial negotiations abroad.

info.memory_trick: fin（结束/界限）+ ance（名词后缀）→ 与金钱的终结（结算）相关 → 金融
info.example: He manages his personal finance carefully to save for future investments.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'fictio'，意为 '塑造、制作、虚构'，由动词 'fingere'（塑造、想象）派生而来。该词根核心含义为 '人为创造而非真实存在'，常用于文学、法律、科学等领域指代基于想象或假设的构建。

info.definition: 虚构作品；小说；编造的事
info.memory_trick: 谐音记忆：'匪客深'——匪徒（匪）和客人（客）虚构了一个很深的故事。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'figura'，意为形状、外形、形态；后引申为数字、图形、人物等含义。该词根强调事物的外在轮廓或可量化的数值表现。

info.definition: n. 数字；图形；人物；体型；v. 计算；认为；出现
info.memory_trick: 想象一个人站在数字形状的轮廓里，既代表‘人物’又代表‘数字’——figure 就是形状与数字的结合体。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'academia'，意为‘学院’或‘学园’，最初指柏拉图讲学的园林，后引申为与学术研究、高等教育相关的概念。词根强调系统化学习与理论探索的领域。

info.definition: adj. 学术的；学院的；理论性的；n. 学者，大学教师
info.memory_trick: 联想‘academy’（学院）加‘-ic’（形容词后缀），即‘学院的’，引申为‘学术的’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his academic focus is on linguistic theory.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'cadere'，意为 '落下、降临、发生'。该词根常引申为事件的发生、事情的出现或偶然的相遇，强调动作的突然性或结果性。

info.definition: n. 发生率；影响范围；发生的事
info.memory_trick: in（进入）+ cid（落下）+ ence（名词后缀）→ 事情落到头上 → 发生率
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: n. 事件；事故；事变 adj. 附带的；伴随的
info.memory_trick: in（进入）+ cid（落下）+ ent（名词/形容词后缀）→ 突然落下的事 → 事件
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: adv. 顺便提一句；偶然地；附带地
info.memory_trick: incident（事件）+ ally（副词后缀）→ 如同事件发生一样顺便 → 顺便提一句
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语前缀 'be-'，意为'使……成为'或'让……处于某种状态'。在英语中，它常附加在名词或形容词前，构成动词，强调使事物具备某种性质或状态。例如，'behave' 原义为'使拥有行为'，'behalf' 为'使在一边'，'behaviour' 则为'行为的方式'。

info.memory_trick: be-（使）+ have（有）+ -iour（名词后缀）→ 使拥有的样子 → 行为举止。可以想象：一个人被要求'使有'良好的'样子'，这就是他的行为。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is reflected in his learning behaviour.

info.memory_trick: be-（使）+ have（有）→ 使拥有某种姿态 → 表现。联想到：在公共场合，父母常教导孩子要'behave'，即'使自己的行为有规矩'。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he behaves with great curiosity in class.

info.memory_trick: be-（使）+ half（一半）→ 使站在某一边 → 代表某人的利益。想象：在谈判中，律师'使自己在客户的一半'，即代表客户说话。
info.example: On behalf of the team, he accepted the award for their outstanding progress.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'dēlātum'（推迟、拖延），由前缀 'de-'（离开、向下）与 'lātum'（带来、携带）组成，字面意为‘带离原定时间点’，引申为‘使某事晚发生’。

info.definition: n. 延迟，延期；v. 推迟，使延迟
info.memory_trick: 联想‘地累’——地上东西太多，走路被拖累，导致到达时间延迟。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, without any delay.

info.origin: 词根 else- 源自古英语 elles，意为“其他、另外”，与拉丁语 alius（其他）同源，强调在已有范围之外的另一种可能性或场所。

info.memory_trick: else（其他）+ where（哪里）→ 其他哪里 → 在别处
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his teaching skills are far better elsewhere.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'fendere'，意为‘打击、击退’。在英语中引申为‘防御、抵挡’或‘反抗、挑战’，核心语义围绕对抗外来攻击或压力。

info.memory_trick: de-（加强）+ fend（打击）→ 全力打击来犯之敌→ 防御
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helps him defend his ideas in international debates.

info.memory_trick: de-（离开）+ fy（=fend 打击的变体）→ 打击后离开→ 不服从→ 违抗
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, yet he continues to defy traditional teaching methods.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'ventus'（风），引申为 'ventum'（到来、发生），因此兼有“风”和“冒险/发生”的含义。在英语中，词根 vent- 既保留了与风相关的物理意义（如通风），也发展出表示“风险、尝试”的抽象意义（如冒险事业）。

info.definition: 冒险；风险；投机；敢于去（某处）
info.memory_trick: vent（风）+ ure（名词后缀）→ 像风一样不确定的事 → 冒险
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes him a valuable asset for any international venture.

info.definition: 使通风；通气；公开讨论（问题、感情等）
info.memory_trick: vent（风）+ ilate（动词后缀）→ 让风吹进来 → 通风；引申为让想法流通 → 公开讨论
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which allows him to ventilate his thoughts in multiple cultural contexts.

info.origin: widespread 由 wide（广阔的）和 spread（散布、伸展）组合而成，字面意为“广泛散布的”，引申为“普遍的、广泛的”。该词起源于14世纪，最初指物理上的大面积覆盖，后扩展至抽象概念。

info.definition: 普遍的；广泛的；分布广的
info.memory_trick: wide（宽广）+ spread（铺开）→ 像一张大网铺开，覆盖很广 → 广泛的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'eligere'（选择），意为 '被选出的人'。原指选举产生的杰出群体，后引申为在社会、专业或能力上处于顶尖层次的少数人。

info.definition: 精英；杰出人物；上层集团
info.memory_trick: e（出）+ lite（轻的）→ 轻（轻松）选出的人 → 精英
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, placing him among the intellectual elite.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 firmus，意为坚固的、稳定的、坚定的。该词根在英语中衍生出与稳固、确认、坚决相关的词汇，强调物理或抽象层面的不可动摇性。

info.definition: adj. 坚固的，坚定的；n. 公司，商号；adv. 坚定地；v. 使坚固，使稳定
info.memory_trick: firm（坚定）如同一个稳固的铁（iron）架，f+irm（iron的变体），想象铁架般坚不可摧。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语词根 'ign-'，意为 '未知' 或 '无知'，与 'gno-'（知道）相对，强调缺乏认知或忽视的状态。

info.memory_trick: ign-（无知）+ -orance（名词后缀）→ 无知的状态。想象一个人对世界一无所知，像被蒙住眼睛。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his ignorance of basic grammar is surprising.

info.definition: 忽视，不理会；故意不理睬
info.memory_trick: ign-（无知）+ -ore（后缀）→ 故意装作不知道。想象别人叫你，你假装没听见。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, so you shouldn't ignore the forecast.

info.definition: 无知的，愚昧的；不了解的
info.memory_trick: ign-（无知）+ -ant（形容词后缀）→ 无知的。想象一个人对常识一无所知，像个原始人。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he remains ignorant of cultural nuances.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'massa'，意为‘大块、团块’，引申为‘大量、大规模’。该词根强调体积、数量或程度上的巨大，常带有沉重、密集或压倒性的含义。

info.memory_trick: mass（大量）+ acre（英亩）→ 想象一大片土地上堆满尸体，即大屠杀。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the historical massacre left an indelible scar on the nation's memory.

info.memory_trick: mass 本身即词根，联想‘massive（巨大的）’和‘massage（按摩）’中的‘mass’都代表‘团块’。
info.example: The mass of the population supports the new policy, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: 巨大的，大量的；大规模的
info.memory_trick: mass（大量）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 大量的、巨大的。想象一座‘massive 山’压顶。
info.example: The company launched a massive advertising campaign, and he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自希腊语，'para-'意为旁边、平行或异常，'lysis'意为松开、分解。组合后原指'身体部分松开或分离'，引申为功能丧失或并行状态。

info.memory_trick: 联想'para- (旁边) + lysis (松开)'，想象身体一侧(旁边)的肌肉被松开，无法动弹。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his fear of public speaking almost caused a temporary paralysis of his vocal cords.

info.memory_trick: 由'para- (旁边/平行) + allel (彼此)'构成，想象两条线在彼此旁边永远不相交。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, drawing a parallel between grammar structures.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词quaerere（寻求、询问、要求），其过去分词形式quaesitus衍生出词根quaes-。该词根核心含义为“通过努力或提问来获取信息或物品”，引申出“探索、调查、请求”等语义。在英语中，许多相关词汇保留了“寻求答案”或“要求获得”的原始意象。

info.definition: n. 寻求，探索；v. 寻找，追求
info.memory_trick: quaes-（寻求）+ -t（名词后缀）→ 寻求的行为 → 探索。联想“骑士寻找圣杯的漫长旅程”即为quest。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, as if on a lifelong quest for knowledge.

info.memory_trick: question（问题）+ -naire（名词后缀，表示“集合”）→ 问题的集合 → 问卷。想象一份印满问题的表格。
info.example: The researcher distributed a questionnaire to collect data, but he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he finished it in no time.

info.definition: v. 引用，报价；n. 引文，报价
info.memory_trick: 源自拉丁语quotare（编号、引用），与quaerere（询问）同源，因为引用时相当于“寻求原文并复述”。联想：你“询问”原文出处，然后“引述”它。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is often quoted by his teachers as an example of dedication.

info.memory_trick: 源自拉丁语quietus（安静的、自由的），与quaerere（寻求）同源，但语义演变为“寻求结束或解脱” → 放弃/离开。想象你“寻求”摆脱某项任务，于是“放弃”它。
info.example: Despite the difficulty, he refused to quit, because he has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly and wanted to master this one too.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'imitari'，意为模仿、效仿，后经法语进入英语，核心含义是复制或仿照某物（行为、声音、外观等）的行为或结果。该词根强调对原型的再现，常带有学习或复制的意味。

info.memory_trick: imi（谐音：一秘）+ tate（谐音：替他）→ 一个秘书替他模仿老板签字。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, often imitating the native accent with ease.

info.memory_trick: imitat（模仿）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 模仿的行为或产物。
info.example: The new system runs in absolute synchrony, not just an imitation of the old one.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'harmonia'（和谐）和古英语 'hearm'（伤害），词根呈现矛盾统一：原始印欧语 *k̂er-（连接、适合）衍生出和谐义，而日耳曼语支派生为伤害义。这种对立反映了语言中同一词源因文化分化产生的语义分歧。

info.definition: n. 伤害，损害；v. 伤害，危害
info.memory_trick: 联想：h-arm（手臂）—— 用拳头打人造成harm（伤害）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his careless remarks often cause harm to others.

info.definition: n. 和谐，融洽；协调，一致
info.memory_trick: 拆分：harm（伤害）+ ony（=only，只有）—— 只有去除伤害才能达到 harmony（和谐）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his team works in perfect harmony.

info.definition: adj. 严酷的，严厉的；粗糙的，刺耳的
info.memory_trick: 谐音：'哈嘘' —— 严酷的老师一说话就让人‘哈嘘’（倒吸凉气），声音 harsh（刺耳）。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but the harsh criticism from his teacher discouraged him.

info.origin: 源自希腊语 'skhema'（形状、形式），后经拉丁语 'schema' 进入英语，最初指‘图表、纲要’，后引申为‘计划、方案’。词根核心含义为‘以图形或结构形式呈现的规划’。

info.definition: n. 计划；方案；体系；阴谋 v. 图谋；策划
info.memory_trick: 谐音‘四给母’→ 给母亲四个计划（scheme）让她选。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his study scheme is still a mystery.

info.definition: n. 时间表；日程安排；清单 v. 安排；计划
info.memory_trick: 拆解：sche（计划）+ dule（=dule音似‘丢’）→ 计划丢了就靠时间表（schedule）找回。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he scheduled two hours of practice every morning.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'imaginari'，意为‘描绘、想象’，与 'imago'（图像、影像）同源，强调通过内心图像构建概念的能力。

info.memory_trick: imagin（想象）+ative（形容词后缀）→ 善于想象的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, thanks to his imaginative approach.

info.memory_trick: imagin（想象）+ation（名词后缀）→ 想象的能力或过程
info.example: Her imagination is so vivid that she can easily picture the scenes in the novel.

info.memory_trick: 拆解为 'I'm age in' 谐音‘我在年龄中’联想到脑中浮现画面
info.example: Can you imagine living in a world without internet?

info.memory_trick: imagin（想象）+ary（形容词后缀）→ 只存在于想象中的
info.example: The story is about an imaginary kingdom where animals can talk.

info.memory_trick: 与‘imagine’同源，想象产生内心图像
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, forming a clear image of the grammar structures.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'margo'，意为 '边缘、边界'。在英语中，词根 margin- 常表示 '边缘、界限' 的概念，引申为 '与主流或中心相对的外围部分'，也用于表示 '余地、差额' 等抽象含义。

info.definition: 边缘；页边空白；差额；余地；利润
info.memory_trick: 想象一本书的页边空白（margin）就像一条边缘线，把内容框在里面。或者联想到 'marg' 像 'mark'（标记），边缘就是标记纸张的边界。
info.example: He wrote notes in the margin of the book.

info.definition: 边缘的；微小的；不重要的；边际的（经济学中指额外单位带来的）
info.memory_trick: 由 margin（边缘）+ al（形容词后缀）组成，意为'处于边缘的'，就像一个人站在群体边缘，不太重要或影响微小。也可以联想 'marginal' 和 'minor' 发音相近，都表示'微小的'。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, though his initial progress was marginal.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'pars, partis'，意为部分、份额；引申为分开、分配、参与等概念，是英语中表示‘部分’或‘分割’的核心词根。

info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ ly（副词后缀）→ 部分地
info.example: His success is partly due to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: 直接记忆：part就是‘部分’的基本词
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and that is an important part of his skill set.

info.definition: 部分的；偏袒的；不完全的
info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ ial（形容词后缀）→ 部分的；引申为偏袒（只看到部分）
info.example: He has a partial understanding of the language, but his ability to learn new languages quickly helps him improve.

info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ ner（人）→ 与你共享部分利益或任务的人
info.example: He is a great partner in the language exchange program, thanks to his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ cip（拿）+ ant（人）→ 拿着自己那一份加入的人 → 参与者
info.example: As a participant in the course, he demonstrated a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ cip（拿）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 拿取自己的一份 → 参与
info.example: He loves to participate in language clubs because of his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.definition: 特定的；特别的；详细的；挑剔的
info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ icular（小后缀）→ 细致到每个小部分 → 特别的、详细的
info.example: He has a particular talent for learning new languages quickly, which is remarkable.

info.memory_trick: part（部分）+ icle（小后缀）→ 极小的部分 → 粒子
info.example: Every particle of knowledge matters, and his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly helps him absorb them all.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语前缀 'ab-' 或 'ob-' 的变体，在古法语中演变为 'of-'，后进入英语。核心含义为 '离开'、'偏移'、'远离'，常引申为 '去除'、'分离'、'产生' 或 '冒犯' 等概念。

info.memory_trick: off（离开）+ set（放置）→ 将某物放置到一边以平衡或补偿另一物。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which offsets his lack of formal education.

info.memory_trick: off（离开）+ spring（跳跃、涌现）→ 从父母身上‘跳离’出来的新生命。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a trait passed on to his offspring.

info.memory_trick: off（离开）+ fend（打击）→ 用言语或行为‘打击’对方，使其‘离开’舒适区。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his blunt remarks often offend others.

info.memory_trick: off（离开）+ fer（带来）→ 将某物‘带来’并‘递出’给别人。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he was offered a position as a translator.

info.origin: 源自原始日耳曼语 *giftiz，意为‘给予之物’或‘天赋’。与古英语 giefan（给予）同源，核心含义是‘被给予的东西’，既指物质上的礼物，也引申为上天赋予的才能。

info.memory_trick: 想象收到一份礼物（gift），你开心地‘给’（give）别人一个拥抱，gift和give同源。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly; it's a true gift.

info.memory_trick: gift是‘给的东西’，give是‘给’的动作，把gift中的t去掉就是give——先有give，才有gift。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he often gives advice to beginners.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语occupare，由ob-（朝向、在……之上）和capere（抓住、拿取）复合而成，字面意为‘抓住并占有’，引申为占据、占用、使忙碌，后发展出‘发生’的语义分支（通过‘占据时间或空间’→‘事件发生’）。

info.memory_trick: oc（朝向）+ cup（杯子）+ y → 把杯子朝向自己，表示‘占据’这个杯子。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his job occupies most of his time.

info.memory_trick: occupy（占据）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 占据你时间的事情就是你的‘职业’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which is a useful occupation for a diplomat.

info.memory_trick: oc（朝向）+ cur（跑）→ 事情‘跑’向眼前，即‘发生’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but such talent does not occur in everyone.

info.memory_trick: occur（发生）+ rence（名词后缀）→ 发生的事就是‘事件’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and such an occurrence is rare.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 scire（知道），引申为系统化的知识体系。词根核心含义是‘知道’或‘理解’，在英语中多与科学、知识相关，强调通过理性认知获得真理的过程。

info.memory_trick: sci（知道）+ ence（名词后缀）→ 知道的知识体系 → 科学
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: sci（知道）+ ent（人）+ ist（从事者）→ 从事知道知识的人 → 科学家
info.example: We must abolish the outdated regulations.

info.memory_trick: sci（知道）+ ent（人）+ ific（形容词后缀）→ 与知道知识相关的 → 科学的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'spatium'，意为空间、距离。在英语中演变为表示两点之间的长度、时间或范围，核心语义是‘跨越、延伸’。

info.definition: n. 跨度，持续时间；v. 跨越，持续
info.memory_trick: 想象一座桥 'span' 跨越河流，桥的长度就是跨度。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his attention span is short.

info.definition: adj. 多余的，备用的；v. 抽出，饶恕；n. 备用品
info.memory_trick: spare = s(剩余) + pare(修剪)，修剪后剩下的就是‘多余的’；也可以联系‘span’的‘延伸’义，延伸出额外的时间或物品就是‘spare’。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, so he can spare some time to teach others.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 pati（忍受、经历），引申为“通过（某种状态）”或“感受（情感/痛苦）”。词根 pass- 既表示物理上的“通过”（如 pass），也表示情感上的“承受”或“体验”，是构建“被动”与“激情”等对立概念的核心。

info.definition: adj. 被动的；消极的；顺从的；不抵抗的
info.memory_trick: pass（通过）+ ive（形容词后缀）→ 让事情“通过”自己而不主动干预 → 被动的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but his passive attitude in class often prevents him from improving his speaking skills.

info.definition: n. 激情；热情；酷爱；盛怒
info.memory_trick: pass（感受）+ ion（名词后缀）→ 内心强烈感受到的情感 → 激情
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his passion for linguistics drives him to study even more.

info.origin: 源自苏格兰语gramarye（魔法、巫术），后受拉丁语grammatica（语法、学问）影响，词义从‘魔法’引申为‘迷人的魅力’，暗示一种令人着迷的虚幻力量。

info.definition: n. 魅力，诱惑力；魔法，巫术
info.memory_trick: 联想‘glam’（闪耀）+‘our’（我们的），我们的光芒闪耀，自然充满魅力。
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语词根 'gener-'，原意为 '种族、出生、产生'。该词根与 'genus'（种类、属）同源，核心含义围绕 '产生、起源、种类' 展开，广泛用于英语中表达与生成、种族、天才等相关的概念。

info.memory_trick: gener（产生）+ ation（名词后缀）→ 产生的一批人 → 一代人
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, a trait that seems rare in his generation.

info.memory_trick: gen（产生）+ ius（名词后缀）→ 天生具有的能力 → 天才
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which some might call a genius for linguistics.

info.example: The portable generator provided enough power for the entire campsite.

info.memory_trick: gener（产生）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 使产生 → 生成

info.memory_trick: gener（产生/种族）+ ous（形容词后缀）→ 出身高贵的（贵族通常慷慨）→ 慷慨的
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he is generous enough to share his study tips with others.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 manus（手），引申为‘用手操作、控制、处理’。该词根在英语中常与手工操作、管理、展示等概念相关。

info.memory_trick: man-（手）+ ipul（拉）+ ate（动词后缀）→ 用手拉来拉去 → 操纵
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to manipulate complex data sets quickly.

info.memory_trick: man-（手）+ age（名词后缀）→ 用手掌控 → 管理
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to manage his time effectively.

info.memory_trick: manage（管理）+ ment（名词后缀）→ 管理的行为或部门
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which makes him valuable in international management.

info.memory_trick: man-（手）+ n + er（名词后缀）→ 用手做事的风格 → 方式
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly in a natural manner.

info.memory_trick: man-（手）+ date（给予）→ 用手交付权力 → 授权
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, fulfilling his mandate as a diplomat.

info.memory_trick: man-（手）+ fest（打击）→ 用手拍打出来 → 显示
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, manifesting his exceptional cognitive skills.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 'illustrare'，由 'in-'（进入）和 'lustrare'（照亮、净化）组合而成，本义为‘照亮内部’或‘使明亮’，后引申为‘阐明、说明’，强调通过光线或清晰表达使事物变得易于理解。

info.memory_trick: 想象一本打开的书，书页上有一盏明亮的灯（illu-）照亮（-lustr-）了文字，旁边配着图画（-ation），这就是‘插图’或‘说明’的过程。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and this book provides a clear illustration of his methods.

info.memory_trick: 用‘路灯’记忆：路灯（illu-）照亮（-lustr-）黑暗的街道（-ate），就像用例子或图片‘阐明’一个复杂概念。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which I can illustrate with a simple example.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语lex（法律）和legere（收集、挑选）。词根leg-在拉丁语中同时承载了“法律”与“挑选”的双重含义：法律源于对规则的挑选与制定，而“挑选”又引申出“收集、阅读”等概念。在英语中，leg-常出现在与法律、授权、水平（如水准尺）相关的词汇中。

info.definition: 法律上的；合法的；与法律有关的
info.memory_trick: leg（法律）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 法律的。联想：leg是法律之腿，支撑社会秩序。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, but he still needs to understand the legal framework of the country.

info.definition: 合法的；正当的；合理的；婚生的
info.memory_trick: leg（法律）+ it（走）+ imate（形容词）→ 法律上走得通的 → 合法的。也可拆解为legit（合法）+ imate，记住“合法身份”即可。
info.example: The government must ensure that all taxes are collected through legitimate channels.

info.memory_trick: leg（法律）+ islation（来自拉丁语latus，带来）→ 带来法律 → 立法。联想：立法者（legislator）带来法律条文。
info.example: The new legislation aims to reduce carbon emissions by 50% by 2030.

info.definition: 征收（税、罚款等）；征集（军队）
info.memory_trick: lev（=leg，挑选/升起）+ y → 从众人中“挑出”钱财或士兵 → 征收、征集。联想：政府“levy”税金，就像从钱包里“挑”钱。
info.example: The city council decided to levy a new tax on plastic bags.

info.memory_trick: lev（升起）+ er（物）→ 能升起重物的工具 → 杠杆。联想：阿基米德说“给我一个支点，我能撬动地球”，lever就是那个撬棍。
info.example: He used a long iron bar as a lever to lift the heavy stone.

info.definition: 水平；级别；水平面；使平坦
info.memory_trick: lev（水平）+ el（小）→ 保持水平的小工具 → 水平仪 → 水平。联想：水平尺（level）确保物体平整，就像法律（leg）确保公平。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and he hopes to reach a professional level in French.

info.origin: 源自原始印欧语词根 *leit-（意为“向前走，引导”），后通过拉丁语进入英语，核心含义为“带领、引导、领先”，强调方向性、先导性及影响力。

info.definition: 领导能力；领导地位；领导层
info.memory_trick: lead（引导）+ -er（人）+ -ship（身份/能力）= 引导他人的能力或身份。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which shows great leadership in language acquisition.

info.memory_trick: 想象一个向导（leader）走在前面（lead）指引方向，记住“领头”就是lead。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which could lead to fluency in many tongues.

info.memory_trick: lead（引导）+ -ing（形容词后缀）= 正在引导的，即处于领先地位的。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, making him a leading figure in multilingual studies.

info.origin: 词根源自希腊语 'phōnē'（声音）、'phasis'（说话/显现）以及 'phainein'（使显现）。这些词根共同传达了通过声音或语言表达、显现或揭示事物的核心概念，强调从内在状态到外在表现的转化过程。

info.memory_trick: 联想 'phone'（电话）中的声音通过 'tone' 传递情绪，就像电话铃声有不同的音调。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, mastering not only vocabulary but also the correct tone.

info.memory_trick: 想象月亮 'phase' 是它显现的不同面貌，就像事物在时间中逐步显现的阶段。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which helped him adapt to each phase of the project.

info.memory_trick: 'phrase' 是说话时使用的固定组合，联想 'phase'（阶段）+ 'r'（重复），即重复出现的言语阶段。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, often picking up common phrases in just a few days.

info.definition: 现象；奇迹；非凡的人或事物

info.example: His rapid language acquisition is a phenomenon that amazes his teachers.

info.memory_trick: 'philo'（爱）+ 'sophy'（智慧），即爱智慧，通过说话和思考显现真理。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, which aligns with his philosophy of lifelong learning.

info.memory_trick: 由 'philosophy' 衍生，'er' 表示人，即热爱智慧并善于用言语表达思想的人。

info.memory_trick: 来自 'sophist'（智者），'icated' 表示状态，即经过智慧修饰后显现出复杂精巧的样子。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, even mastering sophisticated grammatical structures.

info.memory_trick: 'em'（使）+ 'phasis'（说话），即通过说话使某部分显现出来，突出强调。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, placing special emphasis on pronunciation.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'flos'（花），引申为开花、繁荣、流动等意象。词根 'flor-' 直接保留花的概念，'flour-' 演变为繁荣、兴旺，而 'fl-' 形式则常见于与流动、轻快相关的衍生词汇，如 'fling' 中的轻快投掷感，整体象征生机勃勃与向上发展。

info.memory_trick: 想象一朵花（flor）在阳光下盛开（-ish 动词后缀），即'繁荣'；同时挥手（flour）如花枝招展。
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his skills continue to flourish.

info.example: The plan had a fatal flaw, despite his remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly.

info.memory_trick: 花（fl-）被用力扔（-ing 动作）出去，即'猛投'；也像花短暂绽放后凋零，比喻短暂放纵。
info.example: He would often fling himself into learning a new language with abandon.

info.origin: 源自拉丁语动词 tendere（伸展、延伸）及其过去分词 tensus、tentus。核心含义为“伸展、延伸”，引申为“趋向、朝向某个方向”。该词根在英语中衍生出大量词汇，涵盖物理伸展（如 tend 照料本义为“伸向”）、时间延伸（如 temporary）、空间延伸（如 terminal）以及抽象趋向（如 tendency）。

info.memory_trick: tend（伸展）+ ency（名词后缀）→ 事物伸展的方向 → 趋势
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, but there is a clear tendency towards colder temperatures.

info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, and his skills tend to improve with practice.

info.memory_trick: tempor（时间）+ al（形容词后缀）→ 与时间相关的 → 时间的
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year, showing great temporal variation.

info.memory_trick: teen（十的倍数后缀）+ age（年龄）+ er（人）→ 年龄以-teen结尾的人

info.memory_trick: 与词根 tent 相关，本义为“触摸、试探”→ 用舌头试探 → 品尝

info.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ take（拿）→ 在下面拿住 → 承担

info.memory_trick: under（在下面）+ line（线）→ 在下面划线 → 强调

info.memory_trick: un（不）+ employ（雇佣）+ ment（名词后缀）→ 不被雇佣的状态

info.origin: 源自拉丁语 'varius'（多样的、不同的）及 'variare'（使变化）。该词根核心含义为“变化、多样、不同”，在英语中衍生出大量与变化、多样性、差异相关的词汇，同时也引申出“空、虚、广泛、价值”等看似无关却隐含对比或范围变化的概念。

info.memory_trick: vari（变化）+ ety（名词后缀）→ 事物有多种变化 → 多样性、种类
info.example: He has a remarkable ability to learn new languages quickly, showing great variety in his skills.

info.definition: adj. 各种各样的；不同的

info.example: The store sells various kinds of fruits.

info.example: The speed of the wind is a variable factor in the experiment.

info.definition: adj. 徒劳的；自负的；空虚的

info.example: He made a vain attempt to reach the summit before dark.

info.memory_trick: van（空，来自 vain）+ ish（动词后缀）→ 变得空无 → 消失

info.definition: adj. 模糊的；含糊的；不明确的

info.definition: adj. 广阔的；巨大的；大量的

info.example: The universe is vast and full of mysteries.

info.definition: n. 价值；重要性；v. 重视

info.example: The value of this painting is immeasurable.

info.memory_trick: value（价值）+ able（能…的）→ 有价值的

info.definition: adj. 有效的；合理的；有根据的

info.memory_trick: uni（单一）+ verse（转，来自 vers 变化）→ 统一的变化整体 → 宇宙

info.definition: adj. 普遍的；全体的；宇宙的

info.memory_trick: uni（单一）+ fy（使）→ 使变成单一 → 统一

info.example: The workers formed a union to protect their rights.

info.origin: 源自希腊语‘tekhnē’，意为技艺、手艺或系统化的知识。在拉丁语中演变为‘technicus’，最终进入英语，成为表示‘技术’、‘技巧’的核心词根，强调通过学习和实践掌握的专门方法或系统能力。

info.definition: 技术；科技（尤指应用于实际目的的科学知识）
info.memory_trick: tech（技艺）+ ology（学科）→ 研究技艺的学科 → 技术
info.example: The weather in the mountains can be quite abnormal this time of year.

info.definition: 技巧；技术；方法（完成某项任务的具体方法或技能）

info.definition: 技师；技术员（精通特定技术或技艺的人）
